articles.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
articles.summary: Bill Peebles, leader of OpenAI's Sora video generation team, is leaving the company after OpenAI discontinued the project. The departure reflects OpenAI's shift in priorities toward coding and enterprise solutions.

articles.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
articles.summary: Tinder users can now verify their identity through World's iris-scanning orbs to receive free boosts. Sam Altman's World company is expanding this biometric verification service to dating and other platforms.

articles.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
articles.summary: Anthropic's new cybersecurity model Claude Mythos Preview may improve the company's strained relationship with the Trump administration, which previously criticized it as a 'woke' security threat.

articles.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
articles.summary: World, Sam Altman's human verification project using Orbs, is expanding partnerships starting with Tinder to scale its anonymous identity verification technology.

articles.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
articles.summary: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and consolidates its science team, refocusing on enterprise AI over consumer projects.

articles.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
articles.summary: Cursor, an AI coding platform, is in talks to raise over $2 billion at a $50 billion valuation, driven by surging enterprise adoption. a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the funding round.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing legacy platform limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, prompting backlash from developers. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar functionality locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges to recruit engineers. The creative hiring stunt decoded to a puzzle about Berghain nightclub, attracting thousands of applicants.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: Kyrgyzstan-based crypto exchange Grinex halted operations after a $15 million heist, blaming "unfriendly states" for the attack. Researchers confirmed the theft targeting Russian users.

articles.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
articles.summary: Recent advances in quantum computing push toward Q-Day, a hypothetical future when quantum computers could break current encryption. The 2012 Flame malware exploited MD5 cryptography, foreshadowing catastrophic risks from advanced computational threats.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated thousands of VMware customers due to dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware takeover. The competitor leveraged negative customer sentiment around Broadcom's strategy to attract departing users.

articles.title: Pie Day 2026
articles.summary: Ellie organized a Pi Day 2026 event at MIT's Food Institute, orchestrating the baking of 30 pies. The posts document both the celebration and behind-the-scenes efforts of this ambitious baking project.

articles.title: The Download: bad news for inner Neanderthals, and AI warfare’s human illusion
articles.summary: French geneticists challenge the popular theory that modern humans carry Neanderthal DNA from ancient interbreeding, suggesting population structure better explains genetic patterns.

articles.title: The case for fixing everything
articles.summary: Stewart Brand's new book explores how maintenance and repair of tools, systems, and infrastructure profoundly impact civilization. He argues that maintaining things—from motorcycles to planets—is a radical act deserving honor.

articles.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
articles.summary: Researchers developed a fast multilingual OCR model using synthetic data generation. The approach enables efficient optical character recognition across multiple languages without requiring extensive labeled datasets.

articles.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
articles.summary: Ecom-RLVE introduces adaptive verifiable environments for training e-commerce conversational agents using reinforcement learning. The system improves agent performance in realistic shopping scenarios.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: Article discusses how AI systems can autonomously generate pull requests, automating code improvements developers would typically create themselves.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation, exploring emerging trends and potential applications.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for modern agriculture technology. The 'farm of the future' toolkit enables developers to build AI-powered farming solutions.

articles.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
articles.summary: Google's latest tools help travelers plan trips, find deals, and explore destinations for smarter summer travel. The article highlights seven ways to optimize your vacation experience.

articles.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome introduces AI Mode upgrades that transform web interaction through enhanced artificial intelligence capabilities, enabling users to explore and engage with online content more efficiently.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 now creates personalized images using your personal context and Google Photos, allowing the Gemini app to generate pictures reflecting your unique life.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS is a new audio model enabling precise control over AI speech generation through granular audio tags for expressive output.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6 enhances spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding to improve autonomous robotics performance in real-world tasks.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source model designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while other AI developments were discussed.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances real-time photorealism in games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate enhanced details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: OpenAI Agents SDK improves governance with sandbox execution
articles.summary: OpenAI introduces sandbox execution in its Agents SDK, enabling enterprise teams to deploy automated workflows with controlled risk and improved governance while balancing model capabilities with security.

articles.title: Cadence expands AI and robotic partnerships with Nvidia, Google Cloud
articles.summary: Cadence Design Systems expanded AI partnerships with Nvidia and Google Cloud, integrating physics-based simulation and accelerated computing for robotic systems and semiconductor design using Nvidia's CUDA-X and Omniverse.

articles.title: Commvault launches a ‘Ctrl-Z’ for cloud AI workloads
articles.summary: Commvault launched AI Protect, an undo feature for cloud AI workloads that discovers, monitors, and rolls back autonomous AI agent actions across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud to address governance risks.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by answering where robots are, where they're going, and how to get there.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: LLM self-distillation for faster reasoning reduces generalization abilities and limits alternative solution exploration on complex problems.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are becoming the real competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: A End-to-End Coding Guide to Running OpenAI GPT-OSS Open-Weight Models with Advanced Inference Workflows
articles.summary: Tutorial on deploying OpenAI's GPT-OSS open-weight models in Google Colab, covering setup, quantization, and advanced inference workflows like structured generation and multi-turn dialogue.

articles.title: Top 19 AI Red Teaming Tools (2026): Secure Your ML Models
articles.summary: AI red teaming systematically tests artificial intelligence systems against adversarial attacks and security vulnerabilities. It goes beyond traditional penetration testing to identify unknown AI-specific risks and emergent behaviors in generative AI and machine learning models.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Build a Production-Grade Background Task Processing System Using Huey with SQLite, Scheduling, Retries, Pipelines, and Concurrency Control
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a production-grade background task processing system using Huey with SQLite instead of Redis, covering retries, scheduling, pipelines, and concurrency control in a self-contained notebook environment.

articles.title: Your old iPad or Android tablet can be your new smart home panel - here's how
articles.summary: Old tablets can be repurposed as smart home control panels. This simple conversion transforms unused devices into functional smart home hubs.

articles.title: Apple's original AirTag still tracks effectively, and you can get a 4-pack for its best price ever
articles.summary: Apple's original AirTag Gen 1 remains effective for Bluetooth tracking at a discounted price, offering nearly 50% savings compared to the newer Gen 2 model.

articles.title: T-Mobile will give you an iPad for $99 when you sign up for a new line - here's how
articles.summary: T-Mobile is offering a base model iPad for $99 to customers who sign up for a new line. This is a significant discount on the tablet device.

articles.title: The Complete Guide to Inference Caching in LLMs
articles.summary: Inference caching optimizes large language model API calls by reducing costs and latency at scale through efficient data reuse and prompt caching techniques.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article explores using Python decorators in production machine learning engineering. Decorators help optimize and manage ML workflows efficiently.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article discusses five techniques to optimize Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for handling long contexts efficiently, improving performance and reducing computational overhead.

articles.title: Beyond Prompting: Using Agent Skills in Data Science
articles.summary: An author converted their eight-year weekly visualization routine into a reusable AI workflow, demonstrating how to leverage agent skills beyond simple prompting in data science applications.

articles.title: You Don’t Need Many Labels to Learn
articles.summary: Unsupervised models can become strong classifiers with minimal labeled data, reducing annotation requirements significantly.

articles.title: 6 Things I Learned Building LLMs From Scratch That No Tutorial Teaches You
articles.summary: Article reveals practical insights on building LLMs from scratch, covering advanced optimization techniques like rank-stabilized scaling and quantization stability that go beyond standard tutorials.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's PRAGMATA sci-fi action game launches on GeForce NOW the same day worldwide, allowing players to stream it instantly to any device without needing console hardware.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have evolved into AI token factories, making cost per token the primary metric for evaluating infrastructure economics rather than traditional compute costs or FLOPS per dollar.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe announces a new Color Mode for Premiere in beta, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology at NAB Show 2026. The feature aims to enhance video editors' creative workflows with GPU acceleration.

articles.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now offers granular cost attribution for AI inference, automatically attributing costs to IAM principals for better cost tracking, chargebacks, and optimization across all models.

articles.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Bedrock optimizes video semantic search by balancing accuracy and latency. Intelligent intent routing with smaller distilled models reduces end-to-end search time while maintaining strong performance.

articles.title: Power video semantic search with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enable efficient video semantic search, allowing organizations like sports broadcasters, studios, and news outlets to quickly locate specific moments within video content for faster content delivery and monetization.

articles.title: Full-Stack Optimizations for Agentic Inference with NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo enables full-stack optimizations for agentic inference, with AI coding agents now generating production code at scale across major companies like Stripe and Ramp.

articles.title: Build a More Secure, Always-On Local AI Agent with OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw
articles.summary: OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw enable development of secure, always-on local AI agents that perform autonomous tasks like file reading, API calls, and multi-step workflows beyond traditional Q&A systems.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: AI accelerates development of small modular nuclear reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV designs to meet safety, efficiency, and sustainability requirements. This approach standardizes designs and improves project economics for cleaner energy solutions.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Study examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media ads across platforms, conducted in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology through unconventional approaches and diverse perspectives that challenged industry norms.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify feature interactions in large language models at scale, advancing interpretability research to make LLM decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an information estimator to quantify measurement quality based on noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability challenges and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
